meta.title: EU Pay Transparency Directive — Member State Tracker
meta.subtitle: Where the 27 Member States Stand
meta.disclaimer: Snapshot for ISO week 2026-W22 (22 May 2026). Italy is the only status note that changed since W21 (Gazzetta Ufficiale publication watch — see card); all other countries carry their most recent verification. This page does not constitute legal advice.

green.label: Transposed or on-track
green.definition: National law adopted, or final bill confirmed to enter into force on or before 7 June 2026.

amber.label: Draft published
amber.definition: Bill or decree in formal consultation or before parliament; intent clear, deadline at risk.

red.label: No public draft / resisting
red.definition: No formal text published, only initial steps announced — or government has signalled non-compliance.

directivePillars: Pay range disclosed in job ads / before interview
Right of employees to information on pay & criteria
Ban on salary-history questions of candidates
Joint pay assessment if unjustified gap > 5%
Reversed burden of proof in equal-pay litigation

directiveSources.title: European Commission confirms 7 June 2026 deadline holds

SK.name: Slovakia
SK.headline: Equal Pay Act adopted 15 Apr 2026; effective 7 Jun 2026 — first MS
SK.summary: NR SR adopted Zákon o rovnakom odmeňovaní by constitutional majority on 15 Apr 2026; effective 7 Jun 2026. Pay-structure deadline 31 Jul 2026; first reports (250+) by 7 Jun 2027. Includes same-sex equal-pay comparator (gold-plating beyond Directive).

keyDates.label: First pay-gap report (≥150 staff, covers 1 Aug–31 Dec 2026)

sources.title: NR SR schválila zákon o rovnakej odmene
Equal Pay Act passed to implement Gender Pay Transparency Directive
First past the post: Slovakia becomes first EU MS to transpose

SK.weeklyCheckSummary: Unchanged: still the only fully-transposed MS. Awaiting Zbierka zákonov publication after presidential signature; deadline buffer comfortable.

IT.name: Italy
IT.headline: CdM final approval 30 Apr; Gazzetta Ufficiale still not published as of 22 May — 15-day in-force window now at the wire
IT.summary: Council of Ministers gave definitive (esame definitivo) approval to the legislative decree transposing Directive 2023/970 on 30 Apr 2026, after preliminary approval on 5 Feb. Entry into force is 15 days after publication in the Gazzetta Ufficiale. As of 22 May 2026 the decree has still not been published — so the 15-day window for a 7 Jun 2026 in-force date is now at the wire (publication needed within roughly a day). Italy remains on course to be the second EU member state with timely transposition on the strength of the definitive CdM approval, but the buffer is gone and a short slip past 7 Jun is now possible.

keyDates.label: Preliminary approval by Council of Ministers

sources.title: Trasparenza retributiva, primo sì del Cdm
Parità e trasparenza retributiva: nuovi obblighi e sanzioni (4 May)
Pay transparency: servono chiarimenti sulla direttiva (13 May)
Trasparenza retributiva 2026: cosa cambia per aziende e lavoratori

IT.weeklyCheckSummary: W22 watch (22 May): Gazzetta Ufficiale publication still not confirmed. The 15-day in-force window has effectively closed for a 7 Jun date. Holding GREEN on the definitive CdM approval; downgrade to AMBER at the W23 cutoff if the decree has still not been published.

MT.name: Malta
MT.headline: Partial transposition (LN 112/2025) in force since 27 Aug 2025; full draft pending, MEA seeks postponement
MT.summary: Legal Notice 112/2025 amending the Employment and Industrial Relations Act has been in force since 27 Aug 2025 (pay-range disclosure, salary-history ban). The remaining pay-equity provisions (gender pay-gap reporting, objective criteria, sanctions) have not been published. Malta Employers' Association formally requested a postponement on 15 April 2026; the government has not publicly responded. The European Commission rejected postponement at end-April. Malta faces infringement risk for unfinished transposition from 8 Jun 2026 onward.

sources.title: Malta Employers requests postponement of pay transparency directive
Malta implements EU Pay Transparency Directive
Latest developments — Malta

MT.weeklyCheckSummary: Status caveat: GREEN reflects the legal notice in force, but Malta is only partially compliant. MEA postponement request unanswered; no full bill in parliament. Infringement risk from 8 Jun onward for the missing parts.

FI.name: Finland
FI.headline: HE submission to Eduskunta rescheduled to Week 25 (15–21 Jun 2026); autumn scrutiny confirmed
FI.summary: STM working-group draft published 16 May 2025; lausuntopyyntö (consultation) closed 9 February 2026 with final tripartite consultation on 19 March 2026. Government has formally rescheduled HE (government proposal) submission from Week 16 to Week 25 (15–21 Jun 2026) — after the EU deadline. Eduskunta breaks for summer in July–August, so committee hearings and plenary readings shift to the autumn session. Entry into force now estimated late autumn 2026 or turn-of-year 2026/27. Minimum-only transposition: amendments to Equality Act, Equal Pay Ombudsman Act, Statistics Act, Income Register Act and Enforcement of Fines Act.

sources.title: Implementation of the Pay Transparency Directive Delayed in Finland
Ministeri Grahn-Laasonen: palkka-avoimuus ei valmistu ajoissa
Finland publishes draft transposing the EU PTD
Member State Transposition Monitor

FI.weeklyCheckSummary: Updated: Trusaic mid-May monitor confirms HE submission slip to Week 25. Eduskunta autumn-2026 scrutiny now the official baseline; entry into force late autumn 2026 or turn-of-year 2026/27.

NL.name: Netherlands
NL.headline: Raad van State Advice 7 Apr; Tweede Kamer debate 23 Apr, motie vote 12 May; revised bill sent week of 18 May
NL.summary: Council of State (Raad van State) Advice of 7 Apr 2026 finds postponing entry into force to 1 Jan 2027 'legally untenable' — the directive leaves no room for a later effective date. The Tweede Kamer debated the Wetsvoorstel Implementatie Richtlijn Loontransparantie on 23 April; on 12 May 2026 three of four motions were adopted (action plan against pregnancy discrimination; 'maternity-penalty' mapping; wage security in procurement-heavy sectors). Minister Vijlbrief (SZW) announced he will send the nader rapport plus amended Wetsvoorstel to the Tweede Kamer in the week of 18 May 2026. Government nonetheless maintains 1 Jan 2027; Commission rejected postponement on 18 Dec 2025. Infringement risk now goes beyond the Commission's December statement.

keyDates.label: Revised bill sent to Tweede Kamer

sources.title: Advies wetsvoorstel implementatie Richtlijn loontransparantie
Tweede Kamer in debat over wetsvoorstel loontransparantie
Council of State on the Dutch Implementation
Update on Dutch implementation of Pay Transparency Directive

NL.weeklyCheckSummary: Strengthened within AMBER: Tweede Kamer debated 23 Apr; motie vote 12 May (3 of 4 adopted); Minister Vijlbrief sends revised bill week of 18 May — first concrete date since Raad van State Advice. Infringement risk persists, but procedural movement is real.

DK.name: Denmark
DK.headline: Government crisis after 24 Mar election; Poulsen (V) leads royal sondering since 11 May — bill stranded, 1 Jan 2027 endangered
DK.summary: Beskæftigelsesministeriet draft (26 Feb 2026) hearing closed 27 March. On the same 26 February, PM Mette Frederiksen called snap elections; the 24 March 2026 Folketing vote left Socialdemokraterne first (38 seats) but with their lowest share since 1903 (21.9%). Frederiksen failed as royal sondering leader; on 11 May 2026 Troels Lund Poulsen (Venstre, 18 seats / 10.1% — Venstre's worst result in 156 years) took over coalition negotiations. The Folketing is highly fragmented (all 12 parties on the ballot now in parliament). The pay transparency bill was never tabled and cannot move without a functioning government. Working target remains entry into force 1 Jan 2027, but timeline is now seriously at risk.

keyDates.label: Draft bill published / snap election called

sources.title: 2026 Danish general election
Denmark's government talks move from Frederiksen to Venstre
Denmark's draft pay transparency law — what employers need to know
Denmark's Bill to Implement the EU Pay Transparency Directive

DK.weeklyCheckSummary: Major escalation within AMBER: change of royal sondering leader on 11 May (Frederiksen → Poulsen, Venstre). Coalition talks open-ended; the consulted bill cannot be advanced without a sitting government. 1 Jan 2027 target now visibly at risk.

CZ.name: Czech Republic
CZ.headline: Act in force 1 Jan 2027; reporting & joint assessment from 1 Jan 2028
CZ.summary: MPSV draft of 26 Mar 2026 in public consultation; minimum-transposition approach. Phasing: 1 Jan 2027 — pre-employment transparency and salary-history ban; 1 Jan 2028 — joint pay assessment, reporting for 150+, reversed burden of proof; 2031 — reporting for 100–149. Czechia will miss the 7 Jun 2026 deadline by ~7 months; operational obligations only begin in 2028.

sources.title: Czechia charts its own course on transposition
Pay Transparency CZ — Part 1
Czechia charts its own course on Pay Transparency

CZ.weeklyCheckSummary: Re-verified: consultation closed 27 Apr; bill in inter-ministerial coordination, not yet at government. Act enters into force 1 Jan 2027; operationally relevant reporting and joint-assessment obligations apply only from 1 Jan 2028.

PL.name: Poland
PL.headline: Phase-1 binding since 24 Dec 2025; UC127 v2 published 4 May 2026 (74 articles); vacatio-legis switched to 6 months — Phase-2 now Q1 2027
PL.summary: Phase-1 recruitment-stage rules (Labour Code Art. 22) have been mandatory since 24 Dec 2025 for ALL employers regardless of headcount: pay range in the job ad or before interview, gender-neutral job ads, ban on the salary-history question. Phase-2 (Directive Art. 7–12: pay setting, right to information, reporting, joint assessment) advanced to version 2: dated 29 April 2026, published 4 May 2026 by the Rządowe Centrum Legislacji and sent to inter-ministerial and social consultation. Key v2 changes: bill grew from 59 to 74 articles; new wage-structure definition (Art. 10); 30-day clause on failed union negotiations; vacatio legis switched to '6 months after promulgation' — a formal admission that 7 Jun 2026 will be missed. Companion MRPiPS regulation on pay-gap indicators co-consulted. A prawo.pl analysis flags possible directive-conformity issues.

sources.title: Druga wersja projektu o transparentności wynagrodzeń (UC127 v2)
Poland's second Pay Transparency draft: what changed
Poland moves ahead with Pay Transparency
Poland EU Pay Transparency Directive

PL.weeklyCheckSummary: Significant update: UC127 v2 published 4 May, with vacatio legis switched from fixed 7 Jun 2026 to '6 months after promulgation' — Poland formally concedes Phase-2 will slip to Q1 2027. Phase-1 obligations remain live for every Polish employer.

RO.name: Romania
RO.headline: Bolojan government toppled 5 May (281-4 no-confidence vote); transposition bill stranded — deadline politically unachievable
RO.summary: Ministry of Labour completed public consultation on revised draft 8 Apr 2026; text was scheduled to be tabled in Parliament late April/May for enactment ahead of 7 June. On 5 May 2026, PM Ilie Bolojan's pro-EU coalition collapsed after a 281-4 no-confidence vote (PSD + AUR). Bolojan remains interim with limited powers; no successor government in place as of 17 May. Legislative business — including the pay transparency bill — is effectively frozen. Tighter Romanian-specific deadlines (30+30 working days for clarifications) and 30-day timer for right-to-information remain in the draft.

sources.title: Romania revises transposing legislation — key changes
Romania PM Ilie Bolojan's government toppled in no-confidence vote
Romania's government collapse is a trap with no easy exit
Member State Transposition Monitor (May 2026)

RO.weeklyCheckSummary: Downgraded AMBER → RED: government collapse on 5 May removes Romania's path to timely transposition. No parliamentary submission has occurred; no caretaker legislative path visible. President Dan negotiating coalition options; no PM nominee as of 17 May.

LT.name: Lithuania
LT.headline: Government draft 18 Mar; Seimas committee endorsed phased rollout — Phase 1 from 7 Jun 2026, Phase 2 from 1 Jan 2027
LT.summary: Government approved Labour Code amendment (arts. 41, 140, 148) on 18 March 2026; Seimas first reading passed 61–1 (7 abstentions). The Seimas Social Affairs and Labour Committee endorsed a phased rollout: Phase 1 (salary-history question ban, pay-range disclosure in ads, pre-employment transparency) from 7 Jun 2026; Phase 2 (grouped job structures, Sodra-linked monthly pay reporting, peer-group right-to-information) from 1 Jan 2027. Lithuania gold-plates: pay-structure obligations extend to all employers regardless of size (previously ≥20). Final Seimas plenary vote still outstanding.

sources.title: EU PTD in Lithuania: key requirements from 7 June 2026
Vyriausybė pritarė atlyginimų skaidrumo siūlymams
Lithuania delays complex salary transparency rules until 2027

LT.weeklyCheckSummary: Strengthened within AMBER: phased rollout (7 Jun 2026 / 1 Jan 2027) confirmed by Seimas committee. Phase 1 still expected to enter force on time; final plenary vote outstanding.

LV.name: Latvia
LV.headline: Stand-alone draft cleared consultation 9 Apr; awaiting Cabinet approval before Saeima — emergency-track window closing
LV.summary: Stand-alone 1:1 transposition draft published 26 March 2026; public participation closed 9 April 2026. Bill is now in inter-ministerial coordination at Cabinet level before reaching Saeima. No Saeima first reading scheduled as of 17 May. Lewis Silkin's 17 April analysis judged on-time transposition possible only with urgent-track procedure and minimal political resistance; with three weeks to deadline and two procedural hurdles remaining, on-time adoption is now low-probability. Substantive deviations include broader information rights and adjusted reporting timeline.

sources.title: Latvia on track for timely PTD transposition – but with notable deviations
Direktīva par darba samaksas pārredzamību
Saeima Active Bills database

LV.weeklyCheckSummary: Unchanged: bill still pre-Saeima. Probability of on-time adoption now low absent an emergency track.

CY.name: Cyprus
CY.headline: 'Equal Remuneration Law of 2026' still pre-plenary; election 24 May freezes business; government target adoption by Sept 2026
CY.summary: Strong gold-plating draft (broad pay definition, criminal liability, joint employee-rep weighting of evaluation criteria) released November 2025, revised 26 January 2026. The government has publicly conceded passage 'by the start of the new parliamentary session in September 2026 at the latest'. A 24 May 2026 Cypriot legislative election further freezes the parliamentary calendar; the bill has not been placed on a plenary agenda before the poll. Will miss 7 Jun by at least three months.

sources.title: Cyprus sets its course on pay transparency in new draft bill
Cyprus and the EU Pay Transparency Directive: a preview
2026 Cypriot legislative election

CY.weeklyCheckSummary: Updated within AMBER: election 24 May freezes pre-poll plenary business; government openly targets September 2026 adoption — formal admission of deadline miss.

BE.name: Belgium
BE.headline: Flemish public-sector decree in force 7 Jun; CNT President Delarue (15 May): federal private-sector transposition unlikely before 2027
BE.summary: Strong regional asymmetry. Wallonia-Brussels Federation (public sector) has been in force since 1 Jan 2025. The Flemish pay-transparency decree (public sector) was approved 13 March 2026 and enters into force on time on 7 Jun 2026 (≥250 annual, 100–249 every 3 years, joint assessment at ≥5% gap). At federal level (private sector): CNT President Rudi Delarue told L'Avenir on 15 May 2026 'C'est clair, on sera en retard' — Minister Clarinval has still not transmitted a draft to the CNT and a transposition before 2027 is unlikely. Commission infringement procedure now openly discussed.

keyDates.label: Flemish public-sector decree enters into force

sources.title: Directive sur la transparence salariale: 'C'est clair, on sera en retard' (15 May 2026)
Transparence salariale en Europe: la Belgique n'est pas encore prête
Decree partially transposes EUPTD for Flemish public sector
Transposition across 27 MS

BE.weeklyCheckSummary: Escalation within AMBER: CNT President's 15 May L'Avenir interview is the clearest public admission yet — federal transposition 'will be late', realistically 2027. Status stays AMBER because Flemish public-sector decree still enters into force on time.

IE.name: Ireland
IE.headline: 17 May 2026 (RTÉ): Department reconfirms phased approach; Pay Transparency Bill absent from Summer Legislative Programme priority list
IE.summary: Only Article 5 obligations (pre-employment pay info, salary-history ban) are in motion via the Equality (Miscellaneous Provisions) Bill 2025 — Seanad Second Stage May 2025, Dáil First Stage, not yet at committee. Heads of the broader Pay Transparency Bill have not been published. The Department of Children, Disability and Equality told RTÉ on 17 May 2026 that the Bill is not on the Summer Legislative Programme priority list and that employers 'will not be penalised' from 7 June. Ibec and ISME request all non-recruitment obligations deferred to at least June 2027. Existing Gender Pay Gap reporting law (since 2022) remains in force.

keyDates.label: General Scheme of Equality (Misc Prov) Bill published

sources.title: Pay transparency directive — what it means for businesses (17 May 2026)
Ireland: Update on EUPTD transposition and what employers can expect
Employers won't be 'penalised' as Ireland to miss deadline
Equality (Misc Prov) Bill 2025 dossier

IE.weeklyCheckSummary: Downgraded AMBER → RED: 17 May ministerial statement to RTÉ is the load-bearing update — first explicit confirmation Pay Transparency Bill is not on Summer Programme priority list. Phased implementation now official policy; no Heads of Bill exist for the full Directive.

ES.name: Spain
ES.headline: Consulta pública previa closed 8 May (Phase 1 of 5); BOE publication before 7 Jun ruled out — Q3/Q4 2026 most likely
ES.summary: Pre-legislative consultation (consulta pública previa) on the Royal Decree transposing Directive 2023/970 closed 8 May 2026. Under Ley 39/2015, this is only the first of a five-step process: Anteproyecto drafting, Audiencia e Información Pública, CES opinion, Consejo de Estado, Consejo de Ministros — typically 3–6+ months. BOE publication before 7 Jun 2026 is mathematically out of reach. Existing RD 902/2020 (Igualdad Retributiva) and the Plan-de-Igualdad obligation for 50+ staff already provide a partial framework, softening the gap.

sources.title: Consulta Pública Previa sobre el Proyecto de RD
Trabajo lanza a contrarreloj la transposición
Spain moves to strengthen pay transparency
Directiva 2023/970 en materia de transparencia retributiva

ES.weeklyCheckSummary: Sharpened wording: consulta pública previa is only the first of five Ley 39/2015 procedural steps; BOE publication before 7 Jun is impossible. Spain will miss the deadline by an estimated 4–7 months.

SI.name: Slovenia
SI.headline: Inter-ministerial working group ongoing; no bill registered with Državni zbor as of 17 May 2026
SI.summary: MDDSZ (Ministry of Labour) inter-ministerial working group has been drafting since late summer 2024. The Pay-Transparency-Tracker classifies Slovenia as 'In Discussion' (one stage below 'Draft Law'). Tier-1 trackers (Pinsent Masons, Lewis Silkin, Addleshaw Goddard, L&E Global) report 'no public draft' as of mid-April 2026. A 7 November 2025 internal consultation draft was circulated but is not in the mainstream tier-1 record. The 18 August 2025 Slovenian Employers' Association statement urged minimum-only transposition. No bill registered with Državni zbor; adoption by 7 June is mathematically impossible.

sources.title: Slovenia country page
EUPTD implementation across EU member states
EU PTD Implementation Tracker March 2026

SI.weeklyCheckSummary: Unchanged: no bill submitted to Državni zbor; MDDSZ has not published a revised timeline. Substantially behind the deadline.

BG.name: Bulgaria
BG.headline: No published draft; 29 May target slipping
BG.summary: Despite a 29 May 2026 government action-plan target, no draft has been tabled in the Народно събрание and no public consultation text exists as of early May 2026. Realistic adoption window slipping to H2 2026.

sources.title: EUPTD transposition status across 27 MS
EUPTD across MS
EU Pay Transparency Directive in Bulgaria

BG.weeklyCheckSummary: Re-verified: self-set 29 May target imminent without any draft tabled; no Narodno Sabranie first reading scheduled. Zero transposition measures notified in EUR-Lex.

DE.name: Germany
DE.headline: BMBFSFJ in internal consultation; no Referentenentwurf published
DE.summary: Lead ministry is BMBFSFJ (not BMAS); commission report (Nov 2025) under review. Q1-2026 draft target missed; no Referentenentwurf publicly released. June-2026 deadline will be missed. Correction vs W18: lead is BMBFSFJ under Min. Karin Prien, not BMAS.

sources.title: Entgelttransparenzgesetz
Warten auf Referentenentwurf
Implementation of the Pay Transparency Directive — Commission recommendations

DE.weeklyCheckSummary: Re-verified: no Referentenentwurf published by BMBFSFJ; coalition agreement targets info-right from 2027 and a 100-staff reporting threshold. Deadline will slip; minister Karin Prien has not commented in the W19–W21 window.

FR.name: France
FR.headline: Preliminary draft (Title I private sector) to social partners 6 Mar 2026; Title II (public sector) 31 Mar; parliamentary debate not before late 2026
FR.summary: Ministry of Labour transmitted Title I (private sector) to social partners on 6 Mar 2026 and Title II (public sector) on 31 Mar 2026. Mandatory consultations with CNNCEFP/CCFP/CNEN/HCEFH run April–May. The bill goes beyond the EU minimum (lower thresholds, pay range in job ads, stricter sanctions). Parliamentary debate now expected end-2026 — France will miss 7 Jun 2026. Most provisions effective ~1 year post-promulgation.

keyDates.label: Title I draft to social partners

sources.title: France Pay Transparency Draft Law — Key differences
France and EU Commission move forward on Pay Directive
Transparence salariale: projet de loi devrait être soumis au CCFP en avril-mai

FR.weeklyCheckSummary: Re-verified: consultation phase ongoing; no confirmed Council-of-Ministers submission in May. Parliamentary debate end-2026 baseline holds.

AT.name: Austria
AT.headline: No Begutachtungsentwurf as of May 2026; spring-2026 slot missed
AT.summary: Implementation planned via GlBG and ArbVG amendments. As of Feb-2026 commentary, no consultation draft published; no parliamentary registration found by W20. No tier-1 update since W18; tier-2 advisories confirm no draft.

sources.title: Tax News Feb 2026 — EU-Entgelttransparenzrichtlinie
Pay Transparency Austria

AT.weeklyCheckSummary: Re-verified: no Begutachtungsentwurf on the BMA/BMFWF portal; spring-2026 slot definitively missed. Direct effect risk from 8 Jun for employee-facing rights.

PT.name: Portugal
PT.headline: Govt labour-reform proposal adopted 14 May 2026 (PTD scope unclear); no specific transposition bill in Assembleia
PT.summary: Portugal has Lei 60/2018 (national equal-pay framework) in place. No PTD-specific transposition has been published. The Labour Authority (ACT) is already enforcing the directive's spirit. On 14 May 2026 the Council of Ministers adopted a broad labour-reform proposal (Contas Poupança) — whether PTD transposition is embedded in it is unclear and requires direct verification at parlamento.pt and ACT. Aon survey: only 14% of Portuguese companies feel prepared, 28% have done 'almost nothing'.

sources.title: Governo aprova proposta de revisão da lei laboral (14 May)
Portugal: 2026, Looking Ahead
Portugal Pay Transparency 2026: Salary Bands Become Law

PT.weeklyCheckSummary: Possible update: 14 May labour-reform proposal — PTD inclusion unclear, needs verification via parlamento.pt. ACT continues enforcing in spirit.

LU.name: Luxembourg
LU.headline: Avant-projet at social partners 6 Mar; MTEESS confirms 28 Apr (Paperjam): transposition before 2027 unrealistic
LU.summary: First avant-projet de loi transmitted to social partners on 6 March 2026; tripartite meeting 19 March 2026. No submission to the Conseil de Gouvernement. Paperjam (28 April 2026) quotes the Ministère du Travail (MTEESS): transposition before 2027 is unrealistic. Existing strong gender-equality framework somewhat mitigates the gap. Deadline 7 Jun 2026 effectively unachievable.

keyDates.label: Avant-projet de loi to social partners

sources.title: Loi sur la transparence des salaires: les employeurs mis en garde
Pay Transparency: What will change in Luxembourg
EU Pay Transparency Directive — Luxembourg

LU.weeklyCheckSummary: Updated: Paperjam reports MTEESS official line — no transposition before 2027. Avant-projet exists at social-partner level only; no parliamentary path before the deadline.

HR.name: Croatia
HR.headline: No draft published; ministry's March 2026 publication target missed
HR.summary: The Ministry of Labour, Pensions, Family and Social Policy had announced an end-March 2026 publication of the implementing draft (via Zakon o radu amendment) — not delivered. Working group active at ministry level only. Pinsent Masons, Wolf Theiss and PwC HR flag Croatia as one of the laggards. No Sabor scheduling; no Anti-Discrimination Act amendment tabled.

sources.title: EUPTD across MS
Transposition across 27 MS

HR.weeklyCheckSummary: Re-verified: end-March 2026 publication target definitively missed; no new ministerial timeline.

GR.name: Greece
GR.headline: No draft published; specialist committee still preparing framework
GR.summary: No draft law (Σχέδιο Νόμου) or public consultation released. Specialist committee preparing national framework; report expected by 28 Feb 2026 but no public output confirmed. Greece will likely miss the deadline.

sources.title: Greece's EUPTD draft still in progress
EUPTD Implementation Tracker

GR.weeklyCheckSummary: Re-verified: specialist committee report (due 28 Feb 2026) not public; no Σχέδιο Νόμου in Vouli ton Ellinon. Direct effect risk from 8 Jun for employee-facing rights.

HU.name: Hungary
HU.headline: Specialist committee report due 28 Feb 2026; no public draft
HU.summary: Specialist committee preparing national framework with 28 Feb 2026 report deadline; no draft bill or consultation paper published as of W20. No tier-1 confirmation found.

sources.title: EUPTD MS implementation
Pay Transparency Hungary

HU.weeklyCheckSummary: Re-verified: still 'reportedly underway, no public updates' (Pinsent Masons, Accace, Grant Thornton HU). Pre-2026-parliamentary-election politics push the file off the agenda.

SE.name: Sweden
SE.headline: Government still seeks Directive renegotiation; no bill to Riksdag
SE.summary: Government maintains it will not submit a transposition bill to the Riksdag and is pushing for renegotiation at EU level. Commission reiterated in Dec 2025 that all MS must meet deadline.

sources.title: Sweden seeks renegotiation — Is the tide turning against Brussels?
Sweden calls for a renegotiation

SE.weeklyCheckSummary: Q1 2026 timeline: (1) early 2026 targeting 1 Jul 2026; (2) early Mar 2026 shift to 1 Jan 2027 with reporting from 20 May 2028; (3) 26 Mar 2026 full suspension and demand for EU renegotiation. CMS' May-2026 PDF snapshot only captured step (2). EU Commission has repeatedly confirmed (Dec 2025, Feb 2026) that all MS must meet the deadline — Sweden's position is politically maximalist but legally exposed. Watch for infringement-procedure threat in H2 2026.

EE.name: Estonia
EE.headline: Min. Keldo: Estonia will accept EU fine rather than transpose on time (April 2026); partial-only adoption planned
EE.summary: Economic Affairs Minister Erkki Keldo stated in April 2026 that Estonia would pay an EU infringement fine rather than burden businesses with the full directive. Selective adoption is planned only for low-admin clauses (pay range at recruitment, salary-history ban). Estonia formally requests a 2-year deferral plus directive amendments. The 'Palgapeegel' reporting portal is in limbo. Position has political costs given Estonia's wide gender pay gap (among the highest in the EU).

sources.title: Estonia to adopt some elements of EU's equal pay directive
Estonia seeks delay to EU equal pay rules despite a wide gender pay gap
Pay transparency — MKM

EE.weeklyCheckSummary: Re-verified: Keldo position holds; no shift since April. Estonia remains one of two MS (with Sweden) actively rejecting full transposition.
